serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate serving of stir-fried squid with yellow ginger weighs approximately 350 g, typical of a Thai-style main course portion served in a Singaporean food court or restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Squid, mixed species, raw (FDC ID 175168) and Coconut milk, canned (FDC ID 169919), combined with HPB Singapore nutrient composition data for stir-fried seafood dishes
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for stir-fried squid with yellow ginger exists in USDA FoodData Central or HPB Singapore Foodo database. Values are estimated from USDA FDC squid (raw, FDC ID 175168) adjusted for stir-frying with coconut milk, fish sauce, and yellow curry paste, cross-referenced against HPB Singapore data for comparable stir-fried seafood dishes. Input record values (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) are retained as the best available estimate and are consistent with the USDA/HPB composite range. Energy 137 kcal/100g, protein 10g, fat 8g, carbohydrate 7.1g are plausible for this dish type.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — analogous estimate based on mixed seafood stir-fry with coconut milk and vegetables
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for stir-fried squid with yellow ginger. GI estimated at 35 (low) based on analogous mixed seafood stir-fry dishes with coconut milk and non-starchy vegetables from the University of Sydney GI Database. The dominant carbohydrate contributors are coconut milk (low GI) and vegetables (low GI); squid itself contributes negligible carbohydrate. Glycemic load per 350g serving = (35 × 7.14 × 3.5) / 100 = 8.75g, classified as low GL. This is a conservative estimate consistent with the low-carbohydrate, high-protein profile of the dish.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 342.86 mg/100g — borderline high from fish sauce and yellow curry paste
hypertension.guidance: A 350g serving delivers approximately 1200 mg sodium, approaching 50% of the 2400 mg daily limit recommended for hypertension management; patients should request reduced fish sauce and avoid adding condiments at the table.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate 7.14 g/100g, low GI ~35, high protein 10 g/100g moderating glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low carbohydrate content, low estimated GI, and high protein from squid collectively support glycaemic stability; however, patients should monitor portion size as coconut milk adds saturated fat and calories that may affect insulin sensitivity over time.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 4.29 g/100g from coconut milk and cholesterol 100 mg/100g from squid
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: A 350g serving provides approximately 15g saturated fat and 350mg cholesterol, both of which exceed recommended single-meal limits for cardiovascular disease patients; request reduced coconut milk and limit portion to 150–200g to manage saturated fat and cholesterol intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Yellow ginger (krachai/fingerroot) provides anti-inflammatory curcuminoids; nightshade vegetables present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Yellow ginger and garlic provide evidence-based anti-inflammatory compounds beneficial for RA; however, bell peppers and chilli are nightshade vegetables containing solanine, which may aggravate symptoms in sensitive individuals — monitor personal response and request reduced chilli if needed.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Stir-fried Squid with Yellow Ginger (Thai: ปลาหมึกผัดขิงเหลือง; Malay: Sotong Goreng Halia Kuning; Chinese: 黄姜炒鱿鱼) is a Thai-influenced stir-fried seafood dish popular in Singapore's food courts and Thai restaurants. The dish features tender rings and tentacles of squid (sotong) wok-tossed at high heat with aromatic yellow ginger (krachai or fingerroot), garlic, kaffir lime leaves, yellow curry paste, coconut milk, fish sauce, bell peppers, and fresh chilli. The cooking method is rapid high-heat stir-frying in a wok, which preserves the squid's tender-chewy texture while developing a fragrant, mildly spiced sauce with a rich coconut undertone. The flavour profile is savoury, mildly spicy, and aromatic, with the yellow ginger providing a distinctive earthy-citrus note distinct from common ginger. Nutritionally, per 100g the dish provides approximately 137 kcal, 10g protein, 8g fat (4.3g saturated from coconut milk), and 7.1g carbohydrate with a low estimated glycaemic index of ~35. Sodium is borderline at 343mg/100g due to fish sauce. Cholesterol is notable at 100mg/100g from squid. The dish is suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management due to its low carbohydrate and high protein content, but cardiovascular disease patients should moderate intake due to saturated fat and cholesterol. Yellow ginger provides curcuminoids with anti-inflammatory properties relevant to rheumatoid arthritis management. Served as a main course, a standard plate portion is approximately 350g.

GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for comparable seafood stir-fry dishes. Medium confidence reflects the absence of a direct approved-source match.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient breakdown, and moisture data for this specific stir-fried squid with yellow ginger preparation are not available in USDA FoodData Central or HPB Singapore Foodo database as of 2024. Input record source listed as grabfood (a forbidden primary nutrient source); nutrient values have been cross-validated against USDA FDC squid raw data and HPB stir-fried seafood composites and retained as plausible estimates. The dish is sold under menu code 403D on GrabFood Singapore. Country of origin listed as Singapore in input record, reflecting the dish's availability in Singapore despite its Thai culinary origin. Halal status assumed based on typical preparation without pork; individual restaurant certification should be verified. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI 35 × carbohydrate_g 7.14 × serving_g 350) / 10000 = 8.75.
